key_insight: Alkoxy side chains on MOF-5 let thermal expansion be tuned from positive to negative, with chain length controlling a temperature-dependent crossover.
engineering_relevance: Side-chain functionalization provides a practical design lever for programming near-zero, positive, or negative thermal expansion in porous crystalline materials.
method: Lattice dynamics at near-ab initio accuracy
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a length-dependent trend: short chains (n ≤ 2) show monotonic NTE, while longer chains (n ≥ 3) produce a PTE-to-NTE crossover, with further control via linker functionalization concentration.
watch_item: Predictions may depend on accurate side-chain chemistry and temperature-dependent vibrational sampling, so experimental validation across composition and temperature windows is important.
paper_title: Side-Chain Tuning of Thermal-Expansion Crossover in Metal-Organic Frameworks
